description: Pre-existing (confirmed on HEAD 2de4b1a87, 2026-06-12): running cargo test --lib compiler2::drive_test aborts with 'thread compiler2::drive_test::compiler2_native_program_jit_keeps_tail_recursion_bounded has overflowed its stack' (SIGABRT), killing the test binary before remaining tests run. The test intends to prove bounded tail recursion; it currently disproves it under worker-thread stack limits. Unrelated to telemetry work (fz-rh2.19).

comments.text: Diagnosis sharpened at 346ce046d: with RUST_MIN_STACK=2GiB the test PASSES and returns exactly 100_000, so the jitted code is semantically correct — the defect is purely a missing tail-call emission for the self-recursive DirectCall in count/2's second clause (100k real native frames overflow the default 2MiB test-thread stack while RUNNING the compiled code, not while compiling it). Orthogonal to the fz-rh2.21/.22 fixpoint work: drive resolves, lowering settles, codegen compiles. Note the failure mode is SIGABRT (stack overflow aborts the whole test binary), so every suite run must --skip this test until fixed — it is the one thing standing between compiler2:: and being a hard gate once fz-rh2.23's four failures land. Repro of the passing variant: RUST_MIN_STACK=2147483648 cargo test --lib compiler2_native_program_jit_keeps_tail_recursion_bounded

title: drive_test: compiler2_native_program_jit_keeps_tail_recursion_bounded overflows its stack under cargo test
